design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling stages while managing limited ammunition, taking cover in marked doors, and eliminating enemy waves with cooperative partner support
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammunition management) combined with strict hit-penalty system (two hits to health, one hit from bullets = death)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Espionage agent executing covert operations as part of elite WCPO task force; contemporary spy-tech setting with futuristic cyborg enemies
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses to carry over ammunition between levels, forcing constant strategic resource reset; cooperative design where players can deliberately leave ammo for each other via marked doors; identical character abilities despite visual/thematic differentiation

difficulty.lives_system: Two-hit health system per character; one hit from bullets or projectiles causes immediate death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Cooperative ammunition management allows skilled players to optimize resource distribution between two agents; players can deliberately preserve ammo across stage sections

progression.upgrade_system: Two-tier weapon system: default pistol with limited ammo → submachine gun (Walther MP-based) accessible via marked doors; ammo counter visible when entering upgrade doors
progression.unlock_mechanism: Physical doors in stage environment gate access to submachine gun ammunition caches
replayability_hooks: Two-player cooperative encourages different ammo distribution strategies
Contemporary 1990s setting with thematic stage variety (Florida resort stages 1-4, Egyptian pyramid Geldra base stages 5-8)
Female protagonist option (Leila) vs male (Albatross) providing character selection variety

world_building_techniques: Stage environment design (seaside resort camouflaging Geldra base, pyramid installation)
Enemy character redesign (hooded terrorists → futuristic cyborgs reflecting narrative progression)
Thematic staging: contemporary 1990s spy-tech setting replacing original 1968 timeframe
Character differentiation (Leila and Albatross as named agents of WCPO Rolling Thunder task force)

faction_indicators: WCPO Rolling Thunder task force (player affiliation)
Geldra organization (antagonist, contemporary threat attempting satellite destruction)
danger_signals: Satellite destruction plot indicating high-stakes global threat
Cyborg Masker enemies indicating technological adversary
Covert base infiltration (Florida resort and Egyptian pyramid camouflage)
environmental_storytelling: Stage 1-4 setting: seaside resort concealing military installation
Stage 5-8 setting: ancient pyramid repurposed as futuristic Geldra command center
Marked upgrade doors suggest established WCPO supply chains within enemy territory
Weapon variety (pistol vs submachine gun) mirrors tactical escalation
